vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely invalidated by the visual evidence. The pots contain minimal to no visible vegetative matter, suggesting a catastrophic failure of the biome.

inventory_reconciliation.P3_Pothos: Systemic Loss: The white rabbit anchor is present, but the Pothos is absent.

plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor present; soil appears undisturbed but devoid of plant life.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Dark, damp, and uniform; no signs of active root systems or fungal colonization.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of white, crystalline/fragmented material (likely eggshells or mineral deposits) in the central pot.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no organic matter or leaf litter.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-5: Initial state shows a single green stem remnant in the central pot.
temporal_deltas.t-5_to_current: Progressive degradation of the single stem remnant until total disappearance. No new growth observed.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently in a state of total senescence or complete plant loss. The environment is sterile regarding the registered species.
anomalies: Presence of white, fragmented debris (eggshells) in the central pot, which is not part of the standard registry.
Total absence of the registered Pothos, Mexican Mint, and String of Nickels.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a complete systemic failure of the botanical collection. Across the five-day observation period, the remaining vegetative remnants have vanished, leaving only the white rabbit anchor in P3 and some uncatalogued mineral/eggshell debris. The pots are currently empty, indicating that the plants have either been removed, decomposed, or failed to thrive entirely.
